verses.text: Why this proverb, 'The fathers have eaten sour grapes, and the children's teeth are set on edge'? God declares, 'the soul that sinneth, it shall die.' Personal accountability. None may blame his ruin on another's sin.

Why this proverb, 'The fathers have eaten sour grapes, and the children's teeth are set on edge'? God declares, 'the soul that sinneth, it shall die.' Personal accountability. None may blame his ruin on another's sin.

Why this proverb, 'The fathers have eaten sour grapes, and the children's teeth are set on edge'? God declares, 'the soul that sinneth, it shall die.' Personal accountability. None may blame his ruin on another's sin.

Why this proverb, 'The fathers have eaten sour grapes, and the children's teeth are set on edge'? God declares, 'the soul that sinneth, it shall die.' Personal accountability. None may blame his ruin on another's sin.
The just man who does right, oppresses none, and keeps God's statutes 'shall surely live.' Righteousness rewarded. A life that honours God in conduct finds life from Him.

The just man who does right, oppresses none, and keeps God's statutes 'shall surely live.' Righteousness rewarded. A life that honours God in conduct finds life from Him.
If he begets a violent, idolatrous son, that son shall not live; 'his blood shall be upon him.' No inherited safety. A godly father cannot shield a wicked son.
If he begets a violent, idolatrous son, that son shall not live; 'his blood shall be upon him.' No inherited safety. A godly father cannot shield a wicked son.
If he begets a violent, idolatrous son, that son shall not live; 'his blood shall be upon him.' No inherited safety. A godly father cannot shield a wicked son.
If he begets a violent, idolatrous son, that son shall not live; 'his blood shall be upon him.' No inherited safety. A godly father cannot shield a wicked son.

But if that son sees his father's sins and does not imitate them, he shall not die for his father's iniquity but 'shall surely live.' Each judged for himself. The chain of guilt is broken by personal righteousness.

But if that son sees his father's sins and does not imitate them, he shall not die for his father's iniquity but 'shall surely live.' Each judged for himself. The chain of guilt is broken by personal righteousness.
The son shall not bear the father's iniquity, nor the father the son's; 'the soul that sinneth, it shall die.' Justice is personal. God weighs every man on his own account.
The son shall not bear the father's iniquity, nor the father the son's; 'the soul that sinneth, it shall die.' Justice is personal. God weighs every man on his own account.

If the wicked turns from all his sins and keeps God's statutes, he shall live; 'have I any pleasure... that the wicked should die?' Mercy's heart. God delights not in death but in the sinner's return.

If the wicked turns from all his sins and keeps God's statutes, he shall live; 'have I any pleasure... that the wicked should die?' Mercy's heart. God delights not in death but in the sinner's return.

If the wicked turns from all his sins and keeps God's statutes, he shall live; 'have I any pleasure... that the wicked should die?' Mercy's heart. God delights not in death but in the sinner's return.
When the righteous turns to iniquity, his past righteousness 'shall not be mentioned'; in his sin he shall die. A solemn caution. Present rebellion cancels former good if not repented.

They say, 'The way of the Lord is not equal'; God answers, are not their ways the unequal ones? The man who turns from sin shall live. God's justice vindicated. The fault lies in the sinner, not the Judge.

They say, 'The way of the Lord is not equal'; God answers, are not their ways the unequal ones? The man who turns from sin shall live. God's justice vindicated. The fault lies in the sinner, not the Judge.

They say, 'The way of the Lord is not equal'; God answers, are not their ways the unequal ones? The man who turns from sin shall live. God's justice vindicated. The fault lies in the sinner, not the Judge.

They say, 'The way of the Lord is not equal'; God answers, are not their ways the unequal ones? The man who turns from sin shall live. God's justice vindicated. The fault lies in the sinner, not the Judge.
He will judge every one according to his ways; therefore 'repent, and turn yourselves from all your transgressions.' The call sounds. Judgment certain should drive the soul to repentance.
He will judge every one according to his ways; therefore 'repent, and turn yourselves from all your transgressions.' The call sounds. Judgment certain should drive the soul to repentance.

'Cast away... your transgressions... and make you a new heart and a new spirit... for I have no pleasure in the death of him that dieth: wherefore turn... and live.' Grace pleads. God's longing is not to destroy but to give life.
